folio.label: 52v
folio.l: THe Cok of Londou~ , while the Reve spak

note.notetxt: 1The colour is the same of the end of the Reeve: light brown.ODR

folio.l: For ioye hi~ æougút , he clawed hi~ on the bak
A ha q¶ he , for cristes passiou~
This Millere hadde , a sharp conclusiou~
Vp on this argument , of herberwegage
Wel seide Salamon , in his langage
Ne bryng nat euäy man , in to thyn hous
For herberweyng by nygút , is ¹lyous
Wel ougút a man , avysed for to be
Whom that he brougút , in to his preuyte
I preye to god , so ©eue me sorwe and care
If euäe sithe , I higút hogge of ware
Herde I a Millere , betir sette a werke
He hadde a iape of malice , in the derke
But god forbede , that we stynten here
And therfore , if ©e wouchensaf to here
A tale of me , that am a pore man
I wol ©ow telle , as wel as euäe I canö
A litel iape , that fel in oure Citee
¢ Oure host answered , and seide , I gunte it the
Now telle on Roger , and loke æ it be good
For many a paste , hast thow laten blod
And many a Iakke of Dover , hast thow sold
That hath ben twyes hot , & twyes cold
